cds-views-030.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: Production mein Fiori app achanak band ho jaaye toh ek systematic checklist follow karo. Panic mat karo — 90% issues in 6 steps mein resolve hote hain: 1) SICF service active hai? 2) CDS view SE11/ADT mein active hai? 3) /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE mein service registered hai? 4) /IWFND/ERROR_LOG mein exact error kya hai? 5) DCL/PFCG mein authorization correct hai? 6) ST05 se slow queries identify karo. Communication equally important hai — stakeholder ko har 15-20 min update do, silence worst response hai.
cds-views-030.hinglishMasterExplanation:
Urgent support escalation — CDS-based Fiori app down scenario ke liye complete triage:

**Step 1: SICF Check** — /n/SICF → service path /sap/opu/odata/sap/[SERVICE] → Right-click Test Service. Inactive hai → Activate karo. Transport ke baad service kabhi kabhi deactivate ho jaati hai.

**Step 2: CDS Activation — SE11/ADT** — SE11 mein sqlViewName search karo. View inactive ya error hai → ADT mein Ctrl+F3 activate karo. Compilation error hai → dependent view ya table change check karo.

**Step 3: OData Service — /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE** — Service exist karta hai? System alias correct? 'Check' button se connectivity verify karo. Missing hai → Add Selected Services se register karo.

**Step 4: Error Log — /IWFND/ERROR_LOG** — Sabse useful tool. Last 100 errors with exact message, timestamp, user. Service name se filter karo → root cause 5 minute mein milta hai.

**Step 5: DCL/PFCG Authorization** — Kya recent transport mein role change hua? SU53 se affected user ka last failed auth check dekho. DCL aur PFCG values match kar rahe hain?

**Step 6: Performance — ST05** — App slow hai → ST05 trace on, slow action karo, trace off, display karo. sqlViewName se filter karo. Full table scan hai? SELECT * without WHERE? N+1 query pattern?

**Communication protocol:** Har 15-20 min stakeholder update do — 'SICF active confirm hua, ab error log check kar rahe hain, ETA 30 min.' Silence panic badhata hai.

**Rollback vs Forward Fix:** /STMS mein transport object list dekho. Sirf ek problematic change tha → rollback safer. Multiple critical changes in transport → forward fix better. Transport content dekhe bina rollback mat karo.
cds-views-030.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'When a CDS-based Fiori app goes down in production, I follow a structured triage: first SICF to confirm the OData service is active — common after transports. Then SE11 or ADT for CDS view activation status. Then /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE for service registration. /IWFND/ERROR_LOG gives exact errors with timestamps — pinpoints root cause in minutes. For authorization issues, SU53 shows the user's last failed auth check to compare with DCL configuration. For slow apps, ST05 SQL trace identifies database-level issues. Throughout, I update stakeholders every 15-20 minutes. For fix decisions, I check transport content in /STMS before choosing rollback or forward fix.'

Hinglish tip: Sequence yaad karo — SICF → SE11/ADT → /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE → /IWFND/ERROR_LOG → DCL/PFCG → ST05. Real incident example bolo — 'Transport ke baad SICF service deactivate ho gayi, 10 min mein fix hua.' Structured approach + real example = strong answer.
cds-views-030.codeExamples:
" Triage Checklist - CDS/Fiori Production Issue
" 1. SICF: /n/SICF → service path → Right-click Activate
" 2. ADT: CDS view → Ctrl+F3 activate, check activation log
" 3. /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE: Search service → Check → Add if missing
" 4. /IWFND/ERROR_LOG: Filter by service → read exact error text
" 5. SU53: User does failing action → check SU53 Other User
" 6. ST05: Activate → reproduce → deactivate → display trace

" Common error → root cause:
" 'Entity type not found' → CDS view inactive
" HTTP 403 → DCL/PFCG authorization gap
" HTTP 404 → SICF inactive or service not in MAINT_SERVICE
" 'Internal server error' → CDS compilation error

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the first thing you check when a Fiori app suddenly shows no data?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: /IWFND/ERROR_LOG pehla stop hai — exact error message milta hai service name se filter karke. 70% cases mein root cause yahan milta hai bina aur kuch kiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: First check is /IWFND/ERROR_LOG — it captures all OData errors with exact messages, timestamps, and user IDs. Filter by service name to see errors from the incident time. The error text directly indicates authorization failure (403), service not found (404), or CDS activation issue. This one transaction saves significant time versus checking multiple systems blindly.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Monday morning, 20 users reported blank Fiori list. /IWFND/ERROR_LOG: all errors showed 'CDS view ZI_SALES_ORDER not active'. Friday transport had deactivated dependent interface view. ADT activation in 8 minutes — service restored. Root cause found in 2 minutes because of error log.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Error log check kiye bina seedha SICF ya SE11 jaana — time waste hota hai. /IWFND/ERROR_LOG hamesha pehla stop hona chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" /IWFND/ERROR_LOG usage:
" Tcode: /IWFND/ERROR_LOG
" Filter: Service = ZSALES_ORDER_SRV, From Date = incident time
" Read: Error Text column → exact root cause
" HTTP 403 → auth issue, HTTP 404 → service/SICF issue
" 'Not active' → CDS activation needed

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you check if a CDS view is active in production?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SE11 mein sqlViewName search karo — view exist karti hai toh CDS active hai. Quick test: SE38 mein SELECT FROM cds_view UP TO 1 ROWS — success = active, dump = inactive.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Three methods: SE11 — search the sqlViewName (16-char annotation value); if the database view exists, CDS is active. SE38 quick test — SELECT FROM cds_view_name UP TO 1 ROWS, success means active. ADT — open data definition, check activation status indicator. SE11 is fastest in production without ADT connection.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Post-transport: transport log showed partial success. SE11 check of sqlViewName — view existed but showed inactive. ADT connected to production — activation error: dependent DCL had syntax problem from different change. DCL corrected, transported, CDS activated successfully.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Sirf ADT mein check karna jo production mein firewall issues se connect nahi hota. SE11 aur SE38 quick test dono always available hain as backup checks.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Method 1: SE11 → View Name: ZSALES_V → Check exists
" Method 2: SE38 quick test
SELECT * FROM zcds_sales_order UP TO 1 ROWS
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_test).
" Success → active. Runtime error → inactive.
" Method 3: ADT → data definition → activation icon

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the role of /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE in CDS escalation?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE mein SAP Gateway pe OData services register hoti hain. CDS view @OData.publish: true se service generate hoti hai — but Gateway pe manually register karna padta hai. Missing service → Fiori app 404 deta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE is where OData services are registered and activated in SAP Gateway. A CDS view with @OData.publish: true generates the service on the backend, but registration in Gateway is a separate step. During escalation: verify service exists, system alias is correct, use the Check button to test backend connectivity. Missing service causes HTTP 404 in Fiori apps.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: System refresh: all CDS views active, SICF active — but Fiori still 404. Root cause: /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE registrations did not carry over in system copy. All services needed re-registration. Lesson: system refresh runbook must include this step.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: CDS activate karo, SICF activate karo — Fiori still 404. /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE step bhool jaana is a very common oversight, especially after system copies or landscape refreshes.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE steps:
" Search: Technical Service Name = ZSALES_ORDER*
" Select → Check button → backend connectivity test
" If missing → Add Selected Services → system alias
" After add: SAP Gateway Client → GET service URL
" Expected: HTTP 200 with metadata XML

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you use ST05 to debug a slow CDS-based Fiori app?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: ST05 SQL trace — Activate → slow operation karo Fiori mein → Deactivate → Display. CDS sqlViewName se filter karo. Full table scan hai? Kitne rows fetch hue vs display hue? N+1 pattern hai (same query 500 times)?
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: ST05 SQL trace captures all database operations during a session. Activate trace, reproduce the slow Fiori operation, deactivate, then filter by CDS sqlViewName. Analyze: row count versus what is displayed (over-fetching without WHERE pushdown), N+1 patterns from association over-expansion, and execution time. Findings guide CDS optimization — adding WHERE clause, using association instead of JOIN, or controlling $expand in OData.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: PO Fiori list: 18 second load. ST05 trace: CDS view returned 2.3 million rows, OData filtering client-side. Fix: mandatory date parameter added to CDS view, Fiori app updated to always pass current month. Result: 2.3M rows → 4,200 rows, load time 18s → 1.2s.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: ST05 trace production pe unnecessarily long time active rakhna — system overhead. Short trace karo — sirf problematic operation cover kare. Production trace nahi ho sakti toh dev system mein same data volume pe trace karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" ST05 Steps:
" 1. ST05 → Activate Trace (SQL Trace checked)
" 2. Reproduce slow Fiori operation
" 3. ST05 → Deactivate Trace → Display Trace
" 4. Filter: Object Name = ZSALES_V (sqlViewName)
" Red flags:
" Rows = 2,300,000 for 50 displayed items → no WHERE pushdown
" Same statement 500x → N+1 from association over-expand
" SCAN in plan → missing index or no WHERE clause

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you communicate during a production outage to stakeholders?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Initial acknowledge: 5 min mein — 'Issue investigate ho raha hai, 15 min mein update.' Har 15-20 min update do even if no fix. Fix hone ke baad root cause aur prevention communicate karo. Silence worst response hai escalation mein.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Communication follows a structured cadence: acknowledge within 5 minutes, status updates every 15-20 minutes including what was ruled out and revised ETA. Upon resolution: confirm fix with evidence, brief root cause, and prevention measure. Each update in the agreed channel (Teams/email/ticket) ensures traceability. Regular updates prevent panic and parallel duplicate investigations.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Finance app down at 10 AM during month-end. Updates: 10:05 investigating, 10:20 checking CDS activation, 10:35 inactive view found activating, 10:42 resolved. Root cause: transport missing activation step. Prevention: added to deployment checklist. Finance manager: 'Updates kept us calm.' Communication built as much trust as the fix.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Pehle fix karo phir communicate karo — ye approach galat hai. Even 'investigating' message better than silence. Aur fix ke baad root cause zaroor communicate karo warna same issue dobara hone ka risk hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Communication Template:
" T+0 min: 'Issue received. Investigating. Update in 15 min.'
" T+15: 'SICF active confirmed. Checking CDS activation.
"         ETA 30 more minutes.'
" T+30: 'Root cause: ZI_SALES_ORDER inactive.
"        Activating now. Fix in 10 min.'
" T+40: 'RESOLVED 10:42. Fiori functional.
"        Cause: transport missed activation.
"        Prevention: added to checklist.'

followupAnswerBank.question: What is SU53 and how does it help in CDS authorization issues?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SU53 kisi bhi user ka last failed authorization check dikhata hai — auth object, field, aur missing value. Affected user Fiori action kare → immediately SU53 'Other User' se check karo → DCL configuration se compare karo → gap milta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SU53 shows the last failed PFCG authorization check for any user — authorization object, field checked, value the user had vs what was needed. For CDS issues: have the user perform the failing action, then immediately check SU53 using 'Other User'. Compare with the DCL definition to find the gap — wrong VKORG value in PFCG role, missing plant in authorization, etc. SU53 identifies authorization root cause in minutes.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: 3 users saw no data post-go-live. SU53 for all three: V_VBAK_VKO, VKORG blank — PFCG role had VKORG set to blank by mistake. Expected: VKORG = 1000, 2000. Fix: PFCG role updated, propagated via SU10. SU53 identified root cause in 3 minutes.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: SU53 check karna user ke action se PEHLE — SU53 sirf LAST failed auth dikhata hai. Sequence: user fails action → immediately check SU53. Wait mat karo, user kuch aur kare toh last auth overwrite ho jaata hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SU53 Steps:
" Transaction: SU53 → Menu: Authorization → Other User
" Enter username → Display
" Read: Auth Object, Field, User's value vs required value

" Cross-reference with DCL:
" DCL: where (SalesOrg) = aspect pfcg_auth(
"         V_VBAK_VKO, VKORG, ACTVT = '03');
" SU53 shows VKORG blank → PFCG role missing VKORG value
" Fix: PFCG role → add VKORG = 1000 → propagate to user

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you decide between rollback and forward fix during escalation?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Rollback prefer karo: sirf ek change tha transport mein, rollback side effects nahi. Forward fix prefer karo: multiple critical changes in transport, simple root cause, 30 min mein fix possible. Hamesha /STMS mein transport object list dekho before deciding.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Decision depends on: change isolation (how many objects in the triggering transport), rollback side effects (what else would revert), and fix complexity. Rollback is preferred when the transport had only the problematic change and no critical companion changes. Forward fix is preferred when the transport contained multiple working changes or the fix is clear and quick. Always inspect the transport object list in /STMS before deciding — other objects in the transport are equally important.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Transport TR123456 caused CDS issue. /STMS showed: 1) CDS view change 2) ABAP bug fix 3) PFCG role change. Rollback would revert the working bug fix and role change. Decision: forward fix. CDS sqlViewName conflict renamed and activated in 25 minutes — better than rollback side effects.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Transport content check kiye bina 'rollback karte hain' bolna. Rollback se unintended side effects aa sakte hain. /STMS mein transport objects list zaroor dekho. Rollback ke baad import log bhi confirm karo ki rollback complete hua.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Rollback Decision Checklist:
" 1. /STMS → Import history → find triggering transport
" 2. Select transport → Display → Object list
" Rollback YES if:
"   - Only 1-2 objects, all are the problem
"   - No TABU (config) entries
"   - No other critical changes
" /STMS → Transport → Actions → Return Transport

" Forward Fix YES if:
"   - Multiple objects, others are working
"   - Root cause clear, fix < 30 min
"   - Fix → Test → Emergency transport

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle CDS activation errors after transporting to production?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Common causes: dependent view transport nahi hua, wrong transport sequence, sqlViewName conflict, DCL not transported. ADT activation log exact missing object batata hai. Missing objects add karo transport mein, correct sequence mein import karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: CDS post-transport activation errors have specific causes: missing dependent object (interface view not transported with consumption view), sequence issue (consumption view imported before interface view), sqlViewName conflict (another SE11 object with same name in target), or DCL not transported (activates but all users get no data). ADT or SE11 activation log shows the exact missing object. Fix: add missing objects to a repair transport and import in correct dependency order.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: ZC_PO_APPROVAL imported, activation failed — ZI_PO_HEADER not in transport. Error: 'ZI_PO_HEADER not found'. Identified transport containing interface view in dev, created repair transport, imported interface view first, then consumption view activated. Lesson: use ADT Where Used to find full dependency tree before creating transport.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Production mein directly ADT se activate karne ki koshish — production typically locked for direct changes. All fixes via transport. Emergency correction needs BASIS authorization. Never bypass transport process without proper approval.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Transport Dependency Order:
" 1. DDIC tables/structures (if changed)
" 2. Interface CDS views (ZI_ prefix)
" 3. Composite CDS views
" 4. Consumption CDS views (ZC_ prefix)
" 5. DCL source objects (same name as CDS view)
" 6. Service Definitions + Bindings

" Post-import check:
" SE11 → sqlViewName → confirm view exists
" SE38 → SELECT FROM view UP TO 1 ROWS → no dump
" /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE → service still active

cds-views-031.easyUnderstanding:
Simple words mein samjho: FS = Functional Specification (business ne kya maanga). TS = Technical Specification (developer ka blueprint). CDS Views ke liye ye conversion ek important skill hai.

Example: FS mein likha — 'Sales manager ko apne sales org ke open orders dikhne chahiye: customer name, order date, total amount.'

TS mein translate karo:
- Source tables: VBAK (orders), KNA1 (customer)
- Interface view: ZI_SALES_ORDER (VBAK + KNA1 join, association to items)
- Consumption view: ZC_SALES_ORDER_LIST (@UI annotations, @OData.publish)
- DCL: V_VBAK_VKO auth object for sales org restriction
- Annotations: @UI.lineItem for order#, customer, date, amount; @UI.selectionField for date range

Ye mapping — FS business language se TS technical language mein — hi FS to TS conversion hai.
cds-views-031.hinglishMasterExplanation:
FS to TS conversion — systematic 5-step approach for CDS Views:

**1. Data Entities → Interface CDS Views**
FS: 'Sales order data with customer details' → TS: ZI_SALES_ORDER — interface view on VBAK, LEFT OUTER JOIN to KNA1 on KUNNR. Standard table mapping: Sales Order → VBAK/VBAP, Customer → KNA1, Material → MARA/MARC, PO → EKKO/EKPO, FI → BKPF/BSEG.

**2. Filter and Display Fields → Consumption View Annotations**
FS: 'Show columns: Order#, Customer, Date, Amount. Filter by date range, sales org.' → TS: @UI.lineItem position 10/20/30/40 for each column. @UI.selectionField position 10/20 for each filter. Field mapping: Order# → VBELN, Customer → NAME1, Date → ERDAT, Amount → NETWR.

**3. Aggregations/Calculations → CASE or @Analytics**
FS: 'Show readable status label — Open/Confirmed/Delivered' → TS: CASE WHEN GBSTK = ' ' THEN 'Open' WHEN 'A' THEN 'Confirmed' ELSE 'Delivered' END as StatusLabel. For dynamic analytics: @Analytics.dataCategory: #CUBE + @Aggregation.default: #SUM.

**4. Access Restrictions → DCL**
FS: 'User sees only own sales org orders' → TS: DCL on interface view, V_VBAK_VKO authorization object, VKORG field, ACTVT = '03'. PFCG role with correct VKORG values per user group.

**5. Fiori UI Requirements → @UI Annotations in Consumption View**
FS: 'List screen + detail screen with order header and item list' → TS: @UI.lineItem for list. @UI.facet with #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE for header detail. @UI.facet with #LINEITEM_REFERENCE for item table. Association from header view to items view.

**Technical Design document must include:**
- Source tables with purpose and SE11-verified field names
- JOIN conditions field-by-field (VBAK.KUNNR = KNA1.KUNNR)
- CDS layer names (ZI_ interface, ZC_ consumption)
- Annotation plan table: Field | Annotation | Value | FS reference
- Authorization concept: auth object, field, activity, PFCG role name
- OData service name and version (V2 or V4)
- Performance notes: expected record count, parameterization decision
cds-views-031.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'Converting FS to TS for CDS Views involves mapping each business requirement to a specific technical component. Data entities in FS map to source tables — I verify field names in SE11. Display fields map to @UI.lineItem annotations in consumption view with positions. Filter fields map to @UI.selectionField. Access restrictions map to DCL using appropriate PFCG authorization objects — V_VBAK_VKO for sales org, F_BKPF_BUK for company code. Calculated or status fields map to CASE expressions in the interface view. The TS document captures source tables with join conditions, CDS layer names, annotation plan field-by-field, authorization concept, and OData service details. Before development starts, I validate TS completeness against a checklist — every FS requirement traced to a TS component.'

Hinglish tip: Concrete example do — 'FS mein tha plant-wise material movements dikhne chahiye sirf apne plant ke. TS mein mapped: MSEG table, ZI_MATERIAL_MOVEMENT interface view, DCL with M_MSEG_WMB auth object for plant restriction, ZC_MAT_MOVEMENT_LIST consumption view with @UI annotations.' Specific table names aur auth objects interviewer ko confidence dete hain.
cds-views-031.codeExamples:
@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZI_SO_TS_V'
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
@EndUserText.label: 'Sales Order - FS to TS Example'
define view ZI_SO_FS_EXAMPLE
  as select from vbak
  left outer join kna1 on vbak.kunnr = kna1.kunnr
  association [0..*] to vbap as _Items
    on $projection.SalesOrder = _Items.vbeln
{
  key vbak.vbeln as SalesOrder,    " FS: Order Number column
      kna1.name1  as CustomerName, " FS: Customer Name column
      vbak.vkorg  as SalesOrg,     " FS: used in auth restriction
      vbak.erdat  as OrderDate,    " FS: Date column + filter
      vbak.netwr  as NetValue,     " FS: Amount column
      vbak.waerk  as Currency,
      case vbak.gbstk              " FS: Readable status label
        when ' ' then 'Open'
        when 'A' then 'Partial'
        when 'B' then 'Complete'
        else          'Other'
      end           as StatusText,
      _Items                       " FS: Item detail on click
}
where vbak.auart = 'OR'

" DCL - FS: 'Only own sales org visible'
" define role ZI_SO_FS_EXAMPLE { grant select on ZI_SO_FS_EXAMPLE
"   where (SalesOrg) = aspect pfcg_auth(V_VBAK_VKO, VKORG, ACTVT='03'); }

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you identify source tables from a Functional Specification?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FS mein business entities hoti hain. Standard mapping: Sales Order → VBAK/VBAP, Customer → KNA1, Material → MARA/MARC, PO → EKKO/EKPO, Vendor → LFA1, FI Document → BKPF/BSEG. Nahi pata → SE11 mein table description search karo, ya SE16 mein known field value se table find karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: FS business entities map to SAP tables by module convention: SD (V-prefix) — VBAK order header, VBAP items, KNA1 customer. FI (B-prefix) — BKPF document header, BSEG line items, ACDOCA universal journal. MM — MARA material general, MARC plant data, EKKO PO header, LFA1 vendor. When uncertain, SE11 table search by description or SE16 browser with known field values helps identify the correct table and verify join conditions via foreign key definitions.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS: 'vendor invoice details with vendor name and bank info.' Tables identified: RBKP (invoice header), RSEG (items), LFA1 (vendor name), LFBK (bank). SE16 confirmed RBKP.LIFNR links to LFA1. Foreign key in SE11 showed the join field. TS documented: RBKP JOIN LFA1 ON LIFNR, RBKP JOIN LFBK ON LIFNR.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: FS mein 'customer data' padh ke SE11 check kiye bina KNA1 assume karna. S/4HANA mein Business Partner concept bhi hai — BP tables bhi relevant ho sakte hain. Hamesha SE11 mein table structure aur foreign keys verify karo before finalizing TS join conditions.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" FS Entity → Source Table Mapping:
" Sales Order → VBAK (header), VBAP (items)
" Customer → KNA1 (general), KNA1T (text)
" Material → MARA (general), MARC (plant), MAKT (text)
" Purchase Order → EKKO (header), EKPO (items)
" Vendor → LFA1 (general), LFB1 (company code)
" FI Document → BKPF (header), BSEG (items)
" Verify in SE11: Table → Foreign Keys → join conditions

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you map FS display fields to CDS annotations?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FS list columns → @UI.lineItem with ascending position. FS filter fields → @UI.selectionField. FS detail page sections → @UI.fieldGroup + @UI.facet. Amount fields → @Semantics.amount + @Semantics.currencyCode. Fiori Elements app ye annotations padhta hai aur automatically UI generate karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: FS display fields map to CDS consumption view annotations: list page columns become @UI.lineItem annotations with positions determining column order (10, 20, 30...). Filter bar fields become @UI.selectionField. Detail page sections use @UI.fieldGroup with @UI.facet referencing them. Currency amount fields need @Semantics.amount.currencyCode paired with the currency field. Field labels can be overridden with the label parameter to match FS terminology.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS: 7 list columns, 4 filter fields. Annotation plan created in TS and reviewed by business analyst before coding. Developer followed plan exactly. First demo: all columns correct order, all filters present, labels matched FS mockup. Zero rework cycles — annotation plan review saved 2 iteration rounds.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: @UI.lineItem position numbers duplicate rakhna multiple fields ke liye — unpredictable column order in Fiori. Hamesha unique positions use karo (10, 20, 30). @Semantics.amount annotation bhool jaana finance fields mein — currency display format galat ho jaata hai.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you design CDS authorization from FS access restriction requirements?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FS restriction → find PFCG authorization object → write DCL. Company code → F_BKPF_BUK. Sales org → V_VBAK_VKO. Plant → M_MSEG_WMB. Profit center → K_PCA. TS mein document karo: auth object name, field, activity, PFCG role name.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: FS access restrictions map to DCL authorization design in three steps: identify the restriction dimension (company code, sales org, plant), find the corresponding PFCG authorization object (F_BKPF_BUK, V_VBAK_VKO, M_MSEG_WMB), write DCL referencing this object with activity 03 for display. The TS documents: auth object, field, activity, and PFCG role name with example values. The CDS view gets @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK. The DCL object must have exactly the same name as the CDS view.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS: 'Inventory managers see only their assigned plant movements.' TS: auth object M_MSEG_WMB, field WERKS, activity 03. DCL on ZI_MATERIAL_MOVEMENT. PFCG role ZINVENTORY_MANAGER with plant values per manager. BASIS team created roles from TS documentation independently. DCL tested — plant 1000 manager could not see plant 2000 data.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Module-wrong auth object choose karna. Company code restriction: FI mein F_BKPF_BUK, SD mein V_VBAK_BUK — alag objects hain. SUIM transaction mein auth objects by object class search karo. Aur DCL object naam exactly CDS view naam se match karna zaroori hai — mismatch hone pe DCL nahi lagta.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you decide which CDS layer a requirement belongs to?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Interface view mein: source tables, JOINs, associations, DCL — sab consumers share karte hain. Consumption view mein: @UI annotations, @OData.publish — sirf ek specific Fiori app ke liye. Business logic jo multiple apps use karein → interface view. Sirf ek app ka logic → consumption view.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Assignment principle is reusability. Interface view contains: table access, joins, associations, clean field names, DCL authorization — shared by all consumers. Consumption view contains: @UI annotations for one Fiori app, OData service exposure. If a computed field or logic is needed by multiple apps or ABAP programs, it belongs in the interface view. If used only by one Fiori app, it belongs in the consumption view. CASE expressions shared across multiple consumers go in interface view.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: CASE expression for order status initially in consumption view. Second Fiori app needed same labels — moved to interface view. Third: ABAP report also needed labels — no change needed, report selected from interface view and got labels automatically. Lesson: if a computed field might be needed by 2+ consumers, put it in interface view upfront.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Sab kuch consumption view mein likhna — 'kaam ho raha hai.' Problem: doosra app banate waqt logic duplicate karna padta hai. Think ahead — shared logic → interface view, app-specific UI → consumption view.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Interface View (ZI_) - shared by all:
define view ZI_PURCHASE_ORDER
  as select from ekko
  association [0..*] to ekpo as _Items
    on $projection.PO = _Items.ebeln
{ key ekko.ebeln as PO, ekko.lifnr as VendorID,
      case ekko.aedat when '00000000' then 'Draft'
        else 'Confirmed' end as POStatus, _Items }

" Consumption View (ZC_) - one Fiori app:
@OData.publish: true
define view ZC_PO_APPROVAL_UI
  as select from ZI_PURCHASE_ORDER
{ @UI.lineItem: [{ position: 10 }] key PO,
  @UI.lineItem: [{ position: 20 }] VendorID,
  POStatus }

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle aggregation requirements from FS in CDS?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Fixed aggregation (always same grouping) → GROUP BY with SUM/COUNT/AVG in CDS. Dynamic aggregation (user chooses grouping) → @Analytics.dataCategory: #CUBE + @Aggregation.default: #SUM. Status labels / calculated fields → CASE expression. Analytics cube → SAP Analytics Cloud ya Fiori analytical list page consume kar sakti hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: FS aggregation requirements map based on flexibility needed. Fixed aggregation — always show total per company code — maps to GROUP BY with aggregate functions in CDS SELECT. Dynamic aggregation — user can slice by any dimension — maps to @Analytics.dataCategory: #CUBE with @Aggregation.default: #SUM on measure fields, enabling analytical tools to aggregate dynamically. Calculated non-aggregated fields use CASE expressions or arithmetic in the SELECT list.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS: 'Revenue analysis — user can analyze by company, profit center, GL account, time period in any combination.' Fixed GROUP BY would not work — too many combinations. Solution: analytical CDS view with #CUBE, measure fields with #SUM. SAP Analytics Cloud consumed this view — users drag-drop dimensions. One CDS view served all analytical combinations described in FS.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Dynamic analytics requirement ke liye fixed GROUP BY lagana — users cannot change grouping. Conversely: CUBE approach for simple fixed total — unnecessary overhead. FS carefully padho — 'total by X' = GROUP BY, 'analyze by any dimension' = CUBE.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Fixed aggregation - GROUP BY:
define view ZFI_REVENUE_BY_CC
  as select from acdoca
{ key acdoca.rbukrs as CompanyCode,
      @Aggregation.default: #SUM
      sum(acdoca.hsl) as TotalRevenue,
      acdoca.rhcur as Currency
} group by acdoca.rbukrs, acdoca.rhcur

" Dynamic analytics - CUBE:
@Analytics.dataCategory: #CUBE
define view ZFI_REVENUE_CUBE
  as select from acdoca
{ key acdoca.rbukrs as CompanyCode,  " dimension
  key acdoca.prctr  as ProfitCenter, " dimension
      @Aggregation.default: #SUM
      acdoca.hsl as Revenue,          " measure
      acdoca.rhcur as Currency }

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you write the annotation plan section of a Technical Specification?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Annotation plan TS ka wo section hai jo har field ke liye specify karta hai: kaunsa annotation, kya value, kyun. Table format mein: Field | Annotation | Value | FS Reference. Business analyst se review karwao before coding. Developer is plan follow kare toh Fiori app exactly FS mockup se match kare.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: An annotation plan documents field-by-field annotation decisions: table columns — Field Name, CDS Field Alias, Annotation, Value, FS Reference. Cover: @UI.lineItem positions for list columns, @UI.selectionField for filters, @UI.facet for page sections, @Semantics.amount for currency fields, @Aggregation.default for analytics. This serves as review checkpoint with business analyst before development and reduces rework cycles by clarifying UI layout upfront.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS had 7 list columns, 4 filters. Annotation plan reviewed and approved by BA before coding. Implementation followed plan exactly. First demo approved by business — zero UI rework. Previous project without annotation plan needed 3 demo cycles. One day TS review saved three days of rework.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Annotation plan mein sirf @UI.lineItem likhna — @Semantics.amount aur @Semantics.currencyCode bhool jaana amount fields ke liye. Finance-related views mein amount fields bina Semantics annotation ke Fiori mein decimal aur currency format galat dikhte hain.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Annotation Plan Format (TS Table):
" Field        | Annotation         | Value    | FS Ref
" SalesOrder   | @UI.lineItem       | pos:10   | Col 1
" SalesOrder   | @UI.selectionField | pos:30   | Filter
" CustomerName | @UI.lineItem       | pos:20   | Col 2
" OrderDate    | @UI.lineItem       | pos:30   | Col 3
" OrderDate    | @UI.selectionField | pos:10   | Filter
" NetValue     | @UI.lineItem       | pos:40   | Col 4
" NetValue     | @Semantics.amount  | currCode | Currency
" Currency     | @Semantics.currencyCode | true | Paired

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you validate that your CDS Technical Specification is complete?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: TS completeness checklist: 1) Har FS requirement kisi TS component mein mapped hai? 2) Source tables SE11 mein verify kiye? 3) Join conditions field-by-field document hain? 4) CDS view names SE11 mein conflict nahi karte? 5) DCL design security team se review hua? 6) Annotation plan BA se approved hua? 7) OData service name decided? Peer review se 80% gaps milte hain before coding.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: TS completeness validation uses a checklist: all FS requirements traced to a TS component; source tables verified in SE11 with correct field names; JOIN conditions documented field-to-field; CDS view names checked as not conflicting in SE11; DCL design reviewed by security team for correct authorization object; annotation plan reviewed with business analyst against FS mockup; OData service name and version decided; data volume estimated with parameterization decision. A peer review by another developer catches gaps before any coding.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: TS review catch: TS had 'join VBAK to VBRK for billing data.' Reviewer flagged: link is not direct — VBFA document flow table needed as intermediate. Without this catch, developer would have written incorrect JOIN, gotten wrong data, wasted a day debugging. SE16 test confirmed VBFA needed. TS corrected before coding — saved one full day.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: TS mein field names English description likhna — 'Order Number' instead of actual SAP field name 'VBELN'. Developer phir guess karta hai. Hamesha SE11-verified SAP field names use karo TS mein. Ye small discipline hai jo development time significantly save karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS Completeness Checklist:
" [ ] All FS requirements → TS component mapped
" [ ] Source tables → SE11 field names verified
" [ ] JOIN conditions: table.field = table.field format
" [ ] sqlViewName: checked in SE11, not existing
" [ ] CDS view names: ZI_/ZC_ naming available
" [ ] Auth object confirmed in SUIM
" [ ] DCL reviewed with security team
" [ ] Annotation plan: every display+filter field covered
" [ ] @Semantics.amount on all currency amount fields
" [ ] OData: service name + V2/V4 decided
" [ ] Peer review: sign-off obtained

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you estimate effort for CDS development from an FS?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Effort drivers: tables count aur JOIN complexity, CDS layers needed, DCL complexity, annotation count, OData service setup type. Rule of thumb: Simple view (1 table, no DCL) = 0.5 day. Medium (3-4 tables, DCL, annotations) = 1.5-2 days. Complex (5+ tables, analytical, multi-layer) = 3-4 days. Testing = minimum 30% of dev effort. BASIS dependencies (role creation) explicitly note karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: CDS effort estimation uses these factors: table count and join complexity (each join adds 30-60 min), CDS layers required (interface + consumption minimum 1 day), DCL complexity (simple single-org: 2 hours; multi-level: half day), annotation plan size (standard lineItem + selectionField: 1 hour; complex facets: 2-3 hours), OData setup (@OData.publish: 30 min; SEGW-based: 4-6 hours). Add 20% buffer for DCL testing with real users. Document BASIS team dependencies explicitly.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Sprint planning: 5 CDS views estimated using factor model — ZI_PO_HEADER (3 tables, DCL, associations) = 1.5 days, ZI_PO_ITEM = 1 day, 2 consumption views = 1.25 days total, DCL for both = 1 day. Total: 4.75 days. Actual: 5.2 days (10% over — BASIS role setup delayed). Estimate accurate; dependency was unplanned.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Estimate mein testing time bhool jaana — especially DCL testing with actual users having different role assignments. DCL bugs sirf real user testing mein milte hain. 30% testing buffer mandatory hai. BASIS role creation dependency bhi project plan mein capture karo warna sprint gets blocked.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Effort Estimation Template:
" ZI_SALES_ORDER (Interface View, 2 tables, 1 association, DCL)
" - Table mapping + JOIN design: 1 hr
" - CDS development: 2 hrs
" - Association definition + test: 1 hr
" - DCL development + test with users: 2.5 hrs
" - Total: ~6.5 hours = 1 day

" ZC_SO_LIST (Consumption, 6 lineItem, 3 selectionField)
" - Annotation mapping from FS: 1 hr
" - CDS development: 1.5 hrs
" - OData activation + Fiori test: 1.5 hrs
" - Total: ~4 hours = 0.5 day

" Rule: Simple=0.5d, Medium=1.5-2d, Complex=3-4d
" Add: 30% testing + explicit BASIS dependency time
